auth_notes: Requires generation of a JWT signed with a private key (RS256) to exchange for an access token. Often involves tenant-specific discovery URLs and strict scope definitions.
rate_limits: 100 requests per minute per tenant; strictly enforced with 429 headers.
gotchas: Payloads are often deeply nested XML or SOAP-wrapped JSON
Strict IP whitelisting required on the enterprise firewall
Pagination uses non-standard 'marker' or 'cursor' tokens in headers
research_notes: The integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. Because of the JWT Bearer flow, a pre-request workflow or a specific 'Code' node may be needed to sign the token before the main API call. Data transformation will likely require the 'XML' or 'HTML' nodes if the enterprise service defaults to SOAP/WSDL.
